comment.body: Alma has been explaining more since the weekend on posts and chat. Pretty much to my understanding this frees capital from banks and institutions to buy treasuries and support the bond market in the case there is less demand from other countries. The question is if those domestic institutions want those bonds and if Trump will be able to force them to do so and then what would happen if yields just rising. What would that do to the balance sheet of those holding those treasury's.

comment.body: I imagine Warren Buffet must be willing to rollover those bonds he is holding, but how long would he wait to do so. Or what kind of deal he would have to cut with Bessent to get a more favorable yield in the shorter term. In the meantime that takes time to unfold and for now until end of summer good news and additional liquidity has the upper hand. Outcomes are easier to see, but PATH and TIMING, is the hard thing.

comment.body: Alma, can you please elaborate on Powell and rate cuts? What are his options 1. Stay hawkish to avoid inflation and considering yields still went up after his first cuts late 2024, but with risk of rolling over all that debt expiring at much higher rates. Or 2.
